mechanism_type: oxidative addition / CO insertion / transmetalation / reductive elimination
common_conditions: carbon monoxide atmosphere with palladium catalysis
description_short: Pd-catalyzed variant of the Stille coupling in which carbon monoxide insertion furnishes ketones from organostannanes and electrophiles.

transformation_type: Pd-catalyzed cross-coupling of organostannanes with electrophiles
common_conditions: palladium catalysis, often with phosphine ligands
description_short: Classical palladium-catalyzed C-C bond formation between organostannanes and organic electrophiles under mild, highly functional-group tolerant conditions.

transformation_type: intramolecular Pd-catalyzed biaryl coupling via distannanes
mechanism_type: tandem oxidative addition / transmetalation / reductive elimination
reactant_pattern_text: aryl halide or triflate tethered to aryl nucleus + distannane
description_short: Intramolecular aryl-aryl bond formation using distannanes under palladium catalysis to build biaryl and heterobiaryl frameworks.

transformation_type: base-mediated condensation of carbonyl compounds with dialkyl succinates
mechanism_type: succinate enolate aldol addition / lactone opening / elimination
common_conditions: strong-base enolate formation then acidic workup
description_short: Condensation of aldehydes or ketones with dialkyl succinates to furnish alkylidene succinic half-esters after acidification.

mechanism_type: secondary amine condensation then electrophilic trapping and hydrolysis
reactant_pattern_text: aldehyde or ketone + secondary amine + electrophile
common_conditions: secondary amine catalysis or stoichiometric enamine formation followed by hydrolysis
description_short: Use of enamines as enolate equivalents for alpha-alkylation, acylation, Michael addition, and related carbonyl functionalization.
